question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a query with an argument that violates a schema directive constraint, causing OnValidationErrorStrategy.RETURN_NULL to silently return null instead of the actual mirror node record, making a valid record appear missing to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query where a field-level validation directive fails silently via RETURN_NULL, causing a partial response where some mirror node records are present and others are replaced with null, resulting in an incomplete and incorrect export of records?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validationDirectives()] Can an unprivileged external user intentionally trigger OnValidationErrorStrategy.RETURN_NULL on a required field by supplying an out-of-range value, causing the mirror node to export a null record in place of a real one, corrupting the exported dataset?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a query whose computed complexity is exactly at the MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation(200) boundary, causing the instrumentation to reject the query and thereby prevent legitimate mirror node records from being exported?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query that exceeds MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation(200) by adding redundant aliases or repeated fields, causing the entire query to be rejected and all associated mirror node records to be missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation(10) limit by nesting a legitimate record-fetching query beyond depth 10, causing the query to be rejected and the corresponding mirror node records to be absent from the exported output?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the ChainedInstrumentation ordering (MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation then MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation) by crafting a query that passes complexity checks but fails depth checks, causing an inconsistent partial rejection that results in missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user use the preparsedDocumentProvider cache to poison the document cache with a malformed or truncated query string, causing subsequent identical queries from other users to retrieve incorrect or missing mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: graphQlCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a query that is cached by the PreparsedDocumentProvider with a validation error entry, causing all future requests with the same query string to receive the cached error response and miss the actual mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: static initializer (ParserOptions)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a GraphQL query that is exactly at the maxCharacters(10000) boundary, causing the parser to accept a truncated or boundary-edge query that omits fields needed to retrieve complete mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: static initializer (ParserOptions)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a query with exactly 1000 tokens (maxTokens(1000)) that uses all token budget on structural tokens, leaving no budget for field selectors that would retrieve specific mirror node records, resulting in missing records in the response?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: static initializer (ParserOptions)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxWhitespaceTokens(1000) limit by padding a query with whitespace to consume the whitespace token budget, causing the parser to reject a valid query that would otherwise export mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: static initializer (ParserOptions)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a query that stays within maxCharacters(10000) but uses Unicode multi-byte characters to encode more semantic content than the character limit was designed to handle, bypassing the intended parsing restriction and causing unexpected record retrieval behavior?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: static initializer (ParserOptions)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxRuleDepth(100) is set much higher than MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation(10), causing a query that passes rule-depth validation but is rejected at execution depth, leading to inconsistent behavior where some records are partially fetched before rejection?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the maxTokenCount(100) Jackson limit on a legitimate mirror node response that contains more than 100 JSON tokens, causing the response to be truncated or rejected, resulting in missing records in the exported output?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request that causes a mirror node response with deeply nested JSON (beyond maxNestingDepth(10) for reads) to be rejected by the Jackson StreamReadConstraints, causing records with nested structures to be missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the asymmetry between StreamReadConstraints maxNestingDepth(10) and StreamWriteConstraints maxNestingDepth(100), where a response that can be written at depth 50 cannot be read back, causing records to be written but not readable, resulting in missing records on export?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes a mirror node record with a string field longer than maxStringLength(11000) to be silently truncated or rejected by Jackson, causing the exported record to contain incorrect (truncated) data?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the maxDocumentLength(11000) Jackson limit by requesting a mirror node record whose JSON serialization exceeds 11000 characters, causing the record to be rejected and missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxNameLength(100) Jackson constraint by querying a mirror node record whose field name or key exceeds 100 characters, causing the record to be rejected during deserialization and missing from the exported dataset?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: jacksonCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxNumberLength(19) Jackson constraint by querying a mirror node record that contains a numeric field with more than 19 digits (e.g., a large timestamp or balance), causing the record to be rejected and missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a value to the ExtendedScalars.Object scalar type that is interpreted differently by the GraphQL engine versus the mirror node backend, causing an incorrect record to be returned instead of the expected one?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a specially crafted value to the ExtendedScalars.Object scalar that bypasses type coercion, causing the mirror node to query with an incorrect parameter and return a wrong or missing record?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a malformed ISO 8601 timestamp to the GraphQlTimestamp scalar that is accepted by Instant.parse() but represents an incorrect time, causing the mirror node to export records for the wrong time range?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/config/GraphQlConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: runtimeWiringConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an extreme timestamp value (e.g., Instant.MAX or Instant.MIN) to the GraphQlTimestamp scalar, causing the mirror node to return an empty result set and making all records appear missing?
